Milestone 1: Insurance Price Prediction - Data Report, EDA, and Preprocessing¶

In [1]:
from IPython.display import HTML, display
display(HTML('\n    <style>\n    body, .jp-Notebook {\n        background: #F8FAFC;\n        color: #1F2937;\n        font-family: Inter, Segoe UI, Arial, sans-serif;\n    }\n    h1 {\n        color: #12355B;\n        font-size: 2.15rem;\n        font-weight: 800;\n        letter-spacing: 0;\n        padding: 0.55rem 0 0.35rem;\n        border-bottom: 4px solid #2A9D8F;\n    }\n    h2 {\n        color: #12355B;\n        font-size: 1.45rem;\n        font-weight: 760;\n        letter-spacing: 0;\n        margin-top: 1.45rem;\n        padding: 0.35rem 0 0.35rem 0.7rem;\n        border-left: 5px solid #2A9D8F;\n        background: linear-gradient(90deg, #E8F4F1 0%, rgba(232,244,241,0) 78%);\n        border-radius: 6px;\n    }\n    h3 {\n        color: #457B9D;\n        font-size: 1.12rem;\n        font-weight: 720;\n        margin-top: 1.1rem;\n    }\n    p, li {\n        font-size: 0.98rem;\n        line-height: 1.58;\n    }\n    div.insight-card {\n        background: #FFFFFF;\n        border: 1px solid #D9E6EF;\n        border-left: 5px solid #2A9D8F;\n        border-radius: 8px;\n        box-shadow: 0 6px 18px rgba(18, 53, 91, 0.08);\n        margin: 0.85rem 0 1.05rem;\n        padding: 0.95rem 1.05rem;\n    }\n    div.insight-row {\n        display: flex;\n        gap: 0.65rem;\n        align-items: flex-start;\n        margin: 0.25rem 0;\n    }\n    span.insight-label {\n        border-radius: 999px;\n        color: #FFFFFF;\n        display: inline-block;\n        font-size: 0.74rem;\n        font-weight: 760;\n        letter-spacing: 0;\n        min-width: 5.25rem;\n        padding: 0.18rem 0.55rem;\n        text-align: center;\n    }\n    span.insight-label.tech { background: #12355B; }\n    span.insight-label.biz { background: #2A9D8F; }\n    span.insight-label.caution { background: #E76F51; }\n    div.output_png img, img {\n        background: #FFFFFF;\n        border: 1px solid #DDE7F0;\n        border-radius: 8px;\n        box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(18, 53, 91, 0.10);\n        padding: 6px;\n    }\n    table.dataframe {\n        border-collapse: collapse;\n        border: 1px solid #D9E6EF;\n        border-radius: 8px;\n        box-shadow: 0 4px 14px rgba(18, 53, 91, 0.06);\n        overflow: hidden;\n    }\n    table.dataframe thead tr {\n        background: #12355B;\n        color: #FFFFFF;\n    }\n    table.dataframe th, table.dataframe td {\n        border: 1px solid #E5E7EB;\n        padding: 0.42rem 0.55rem;\n        font-size: 0.86rem;\n    }\n    table.dataframe tbody tr:nth-child(even) {\n        background: #F3F8FA;\n    }\n    div.input_area {\n        border-radius: 8px;\n        border: 1px solid #D9E6EF;\n    }\n    code {\n        color: #12355B;\n    }\n    </style>\n    '))

1. Project Context and Business Objective¶

The capstone objective is to predict insurance_cost from applicant health, lifestyle, habit, demographic, medical-history, and insurance-history variables. Milestone 1 focuses on data understanding, EDA, preprocessing decisions, and evidence-backed business insight before the Milestone 2 modeling workflow.

Technical Milestone 1 is treated as the analytical foundation: data quality, target behavior, missingness, and important relationships are documented before model fitting.
Business A pricing model is useful only when the data quality, target meaning, and main applicant cost drivers are clear.

2. Import Libraries and Set Paths¶

This setup cell only imports packages, resolves the project path, defines shared constants, and creates output folders. Workflow-specific helper functions are kept inside the cells where they are first used.

In [2]:
import json
from pathlib import Path

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
from IPython.display import Image, Markdown, display

ROOT = Path.cwd()
if not (ROOT / "Insurance Data.csv").exists():
    ROOT = ROOT.parent

OUTPUTS = ROOT / "outputs"
TABLE_DIR = ROOT / "outputs" / "tables"
FIG_DIR = ROOT / "outputs" / "figures"
MODEL_DIR = ROOT / "outputs" / "models"
REPORT_DIR = ROOT / "outputs" / "reports"
NOTEBOOK_DIR = ROOT / "notebooks"
OUTPUT_NOTEBOOK_DIR = ROOT / "outputs" / "notebooks"

TARGET = "insurance_cost"
RANDOM_STATE = 42
PRIMARY = "#12355B"
ACCENT = "#2A9D8F"
WARN = "#E76F51"
MUTED = "#6B7280"
SECONDARY = "#457B9D"
GOLD = "#E9C46A"
GREEN = "#8AB17D"
PLUM = "#6D597A"
SKY = "#5DADE2"
PLOT_BG = "#FBFCFE"
GRID = "#E5E7EB"
TEXT = "#1F2937"
CHART_PALETTE = [ACCENT, SECONDARY, WARN, GOLD, GREEN, PLUM, SKY, "#F4A261"]

for directory in [OUTPUTS, FIG_DIR, TABLE_DIR, MODEL_DIR, REPORT_DIR, NOTEBOOK_DIR, OUTPUT_NOTEBOOK_DIR]:
    directory.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
pd.set_option("display.max_columns", 80)
pd.set_option("display.width", 140)
print(ROOT)

C:\Users\anupk\Documents\PGP\Capstone
Technical The notebook resolves the project root and creates output folders without loading a large helper block at the top.
Business The notebook can be executed from the supplied dataset without pre-generated EDA tables, figures, or external helper scripts.

3. Load Raw Dataset¶

In [3]:
raw_df = pd.read_csv(ROOT / "Insurance Data.csv")
raw_df.shape, raw_df.size

((25000, 24), 600000)

Technical The raw file loads successfully with 25,000 rows and 24 original columns, and visual inspection confirms mixed numeric and categorical applicant fields.
Business The dataset is large enough for supervised learning while still small enough for transparent EDA and report-level validation.

Technical The initial inspection finds no duplicate rows, a unique `applicant_id`, and missing values only in BMI and admission-year fields.
Business The data can move forward after targeted preprocessing rather than broad row deletion or aggressive cleanup.

Define cleanup helpers used in this section¶

In [7]:
import pandas as pd

COLUMN_RENAMES = {
    "regular_checkup_lasy_year": "regular_checkup_last_year",
    "heart_decs_history": "heart_disease_history",
    "other_major_decs_history": "other_major_disease_history",
}

RAW_MODEL_COLUMNS = [
    "years_of_insurance_with_us",
    "regular_checkup_last_year",
    "adventure_sports",
    "Occupation",
    "visited_doctor_last_1_year",
    "cholesterol_level",
    "daily_avg_steps",
    "age",
    "heart_disease_history",
    "other_major_disease_history",
    "Gender",
    "avg_glucose_level",
    "bmi",
    "smoking_status",
    "Year_last_admitted",
    "Location",
    "weight",
    "covered_by_any_other_company",
    "Alcohol",
    "exercise",
    "weight_change_in_last_one_year",
    "fat_percentage",
]

def clean_column_names(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Return a copy with consistent project column names and categorical typos fixed."""
    cleaned = df.copy()
    cleaned = cleaned.rename(columns=COLUMN_RENAMES)
    if "Occupation" in cleaned.columns:
        cleaned["Occupation"] = cleaned["Occupation"].replace({"Salried": "Salaried"})
    return cleaned

5. Data Dictionary and Variable Understanding¶

    }
)

In [9]:
cleaned_df = clean_column_names(raw_df)
data_dictionary = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "column": raw_df.columns,
        "clean_column": cleaned_df.columns,
        "dtype": raw_df.dtypes.astype(str).values,
        "missing": raw_df.isna().sum().values,
        "missing_pct": (raw_df.isna().mean().values * 100).round(2),
        "unique_values": [raw_df[c].nunique(dropna=True) for c in raw_df.columns],
    }
)

def analysis_type(clean_col: str) -> str:
    if clean_col == "applicant_id":
        return "identifier"
    if clean_col == TARGET:
        return "discrete numeric quote grid / regression target"
    if clean_col in {"years_of_insurance_with_us", "regular_checkup_last_year", "visited_doctor_last_1_year", "weight_change_in_last_one_year"}:
        return "ordinal/count numeric"
    if clean_col in {"adventure_sports", "heart_disease_history", "other_major_disease_history"}:
        return "binary flag"
    if pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(cleaned_df[clean_col]):
        return "continuous numeric"
    return "nominal categorical"

def role(clean_col: str) -> str:
    if clean_col == "applicant_id":
        return "audit identifier - excluded from model"
    if clean_col == TARGET:
        return "target"
    return "candidate predictor"

variable_types = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "original_column": raw_df.columns,
        "clean_column": cleaned_df.columns,
        "analysis_type": [analysis_type(col) for col in cleaned_df.columns],
        "role": [role(col) for col in cleaned_df.columns],
        "preprocessing_action": [
            "drop from modeling" if col == "applicant_id" else
            "target grid analysis" if col == TARGET else
            "clean typo and use as candidate predictor" if col in RAW_MODEL_COLUMNS else
            "review"
            for col in cleaned_df.columns
        ],
    }
)
data_dictionary.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "data_dictionary_summary.csv", index=False)
variable_types.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "variable_type_classification.csv", index=False)
display(data_dictionary)
display(variable_types[["original_column", "clean_column", "analysis_type", "role", "preprocessing_action"]])

Technical The variable classification distinguishes identifiers, the quote-grid target, continuous numeric features, ordinal numeric features, binary flags, and nominal categories.
Business `applicant_id` is retained for audit only and excluded from modeling and the Streamlit schema.

6. Column Renaming and Data Cleanup¶

In [10]:
cleaned_df = clean_column_names(raw_df)
renaming = pd.DataFrame({"raw_column": raw_df.columns, "clean_column": cleaned_df.columns})
renaming["changed"] = renaming["raw_column"] != renaming["clean_column"]
renaming.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "renaming_and_cleanup.csv", index=False)
display(renaming)

14 smoking_status smoking_status False
15 Year_last_admitted Year_last_admitted False
16 Location Location False
17 weight weight False
18 covered_by_any_other_company covered_by_any_other_company False
19 Alcohol Alcohol False
20 exercise exercise False
21 weight_change_in_last_one_year weight_change_in_last_one_year False
22 fat_percentage fat_percentage False
23 insurance_cost insurance_cost False
Technical The cleanup normalizes misspelled source columns and category labels while preserving the original data meaning.
Business Clean names reduce downstream coding errors and make the final app schema easier to inspect.

7. Dataset Profile and Descriptive Statistics¶

In [11]:
categorical_cols = [
    col for col in raw_df.columns
    if not pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(raw_df[col])
]
cat_rows = []
for col in categorical_cols:
    for category, count in raw_df[col].value_counts(dropna=False).items():
        cat_rows.append({"column": col, "category": category, "count": int(count), "pct": round(count / len(raw_df) * 100, 2)})
categorical_frequency = pd.DataFrame(cat_rows)
categorical_frequency.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "categorical_frequency.csv", index=False)

8. Missing Value Analysis¶

In [12]:
def ensure_dirs() -> None:
    for directory in [FIG_DIR, TABLE_DIR, MODEL_DIR, REPORT_DIR, NOTEBOOK_DIR, OUTPUT_NOTEBOOK_DIR]:
        directory.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

def write_csv(df: pd.DataFrame, name: str, index: bool = False) -> Path:
    path = TABLE_DIR / name
    df.to_csv(path, index=index)
    return path

def set_visual_theme() -> None:
    sns.set_theme(
        style="whitegrid",
        context="notebook",
        font="DejaVu Sans",
        rc={
            "axes.facecolor": PLOT_BG,
            "figure.facecolor": PLOT_BG,
            "axes.edgecolor": GRID,
            "axes.labelcolor": MUTED,
            "axes.titlecolor": PRIMARY,
            "axes.titleweight": "bold",
            "grid.color": GRID,
            "grid.linewidth": 0.7,
            "grid.alpha": 0.75,
            "xtick.color": TEXT,
            "ytick.color": TEXT,
            "text.color": TEXT,
        },
    )
    sns.set_palette(CHART_PALETTE)

def bar_palette(n: int) -> list[str]:
    return [CHART_PALETTE[i % len(CHART_PALETTE)] for i in range(max(n, 1))]

def style_current_figure() -> None:
    fig = plt.gcf()
    fig.patch.set_facecolor(PLOT_BG)
    for ax in fig.axes:
        ax.set_facecolor(PLOT_BG)
        ax.title.set_fontweight("bold")
        ax.title.set_color(PRIMARY)
        ax.xaxis.label.set_color(MUTED)
        ax.yaxis.label.set_color(MUTED)
        ax.tick_params(axis="both", colors=TEXT, labelsize=8.5)
        ax.grid(True, color=GRID, linewidth=0.7, alpha=0.75)
        for side in ["top", "right"]:
            ax.spines[side].set_visible(False)
        for side in ["left", "bottom"]:
            ax.spines[side].set_color(GRID)
            ax.spines[side].set_linewidth(0.8)

def rel_path(path: Path) -> str:
    return str(path.resolve().relative_to(ROOT)).replace("\\", "/")

def savefig(path: Path) -> None:
    style_current_figure()
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig(path, dpi=180, bbox_inches="tight")
    plt.close()

ensure_dirs()
set_visual_theme()

missing_values = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "column": raw_df.columns,
        "missing_count": raw_df.isna().sum().values,
        "missing_pct": (raw_df.isna().mean().values * 100).round(2),
    }
).sort_values("missing_count", ascending=False)
missing_values.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "missing_values.csv", index=False)

missing_bmi = raw_df.assign(bmi_missing=raw_df["bmi"].isna()).groupby("bmi_missing")["insurance_cost"].agg(["count", "mean", "median"]).reset_index()
missing_admission = raw_df.assign(year_last_admitted_missing=raw_df["Year_last_admitted"].isna()).groupby("year_last_admitted_missing")["insurance_cost"].agg(["count", "mean", "median"]).reset_index()
missing_bmi.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "missing_bmi_profile.csv", index=False)
missing_admission.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "missing_year_last_admitted_profile.csv", index=False)

plot_missing = missing_values[missing_values["missing_count"] > 0]
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4.2))
sns.barplot(data=plot_missing, x="missing_count", y="column", hue="column", palette=bar_palette(len(plot_missing)), legend=False)
plt.title("Missing Values by Column")
plt.xlabel("Missing rows")
plt.ylabel("")
savefig(FIG_DIR / "missing_values.png")

impact_rows = []
for column in ["bmi", "Year_last_admitted"]:
    flag = raw_df[column].isna()
    impact_rows.append({"column": column, "missing_status": "present", "mean_cost": raw_df.loc[~flag, TARGET].mean()})
    impact_rows.append({"column": column, "missing_status": "missing", "mean_cost": raw_df.loc[flag, TARGET].mean()})
missing_impact = pd.DataFrame(impact_rows)
plt.figure(figsize=(7.4, 4.4))
sns.barplot(data=missing_impact, x="column", y="mean_cost", hue="missing_status", palette=[ACCENT, WARN])
plt.title("Insurance Cost by Missingness Status")
plt.xlabel("")
plt.ylabel("Mean insurance cost")
savefig(FIG_DIR / "missingness_target_impact.png")

display(missing_values)
display(missing_bmi)
display(missing_admission)

Target impact of missingness

Technical BMI missingness is limited, but Year_last_admitted missingness is substantial and structurally meaningful.
Business The model should preserve missingness indicators because missing admission history changes customer context.
Technical The missingness-impact chart shows target mean differences between present and missing groups.
Business This explains why the pipeline uses flags and imputation instead of dropping incomplete records.

9. Duplicate and Unwanted Variable Analysis¶

In [15]:
duplicate_summary = pd.DataFrame(
    {
        "check": ["duplicate_rows", "unique_applicant_id", "applicant_id_in_model_columns"],
        "value": [int(raw_df.duplicated().sum()), int(raw_df["applicant_id"].nunique()), "applicant_id" in RAW_MODEL_COLUMNS],
    }
)
duplicate_summary
Out[15]:
check value
0 duplicate_rows 0
1 unique_applicant_id 25000
2 applicant_id_in_model_columns False
Technical `applicant_id` is unique for every row and is not included in the modeling feature list.
Business Removing the identifier avoids memorization and supports a deployable applicant-input form.

Target boxplot

Technical The target is mildly right-skewed, with valid high-cost values that should remain in the dataset.
Business High-cost applicants represent legitimate pricing cases, not noise to be removed.
Technical The boxplot identifies upper-tail premiums, but these align with valid quote bands rather than impossible values.
Business The business needs accurate estimates for expensive cases, so target outlier removal would be harmful.

11. Target Pricing Grid Analysis¶

In [19]:
def target_grid_metadata(y: pd.Series) -> dict[str, object]:
    values = pd.Series(y).dropna().astype(float)
    levels = np.sort(values.unique())
    int_levels = np.rint(levels).astype(int)
    diffs = np.diff(int_levels)
    positive_diffs = diffs[diffs > 0]
    grid_step = int(np.gcd.reduce(positive_diffs)) if len(positive_diffs) else 0
    return {
        "target_unique_count": int(len(int_levels)),
        "target_grid_step": grid_step,
        "target_min": int(int_levels.min()) if len(int_levels) else None,
        "target_max": int(int_levels.max()) if len(int_levels) else None,
        "valid_target_levels": int_levels.tolist(),
    }

grid_meta = target_grid_metadata(raw_df[TARGET])
target_grid = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        ["target_unique_count", grid_meta["target_unique_count"]],
        ["target_grid_step", grid_meta["target_grid_step"]],
        ["target_min", grid_meta["target_min"]],
        ["target_max", grid_meta["target_max"]],
        ["first_10_levels", ", ".join(map(str, grid_meta["valid_target_levels"][:10]))],
        ["last_10_levels", ", ".join(map(str, grid_meta["valid_target_levels"][-10:]))],
    ],
    columns=["metric", "value"],
)
target_frequency = raw_df[TARGET].value_counts().sort_index().rename_axis("insurance_cost").reset_index(name="customer_count")
target_frequency["pct"] = (target_frequency["customer_count"] / len(raw_df) * 100).round(3)
target_grid.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "target_grid_summary.csv", index=False)
target_frequency.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "target_price_band_frequency.csv", index=False)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 4.8))
sns.barplot(data=target_frequency, x="insurance_cost", y="customer_count", color=SECONDARY)
plt.title("Customer Count by Insurance Quote Band")
plt.xlabel("insurance_cost quote band")
plt.ylabel("Customer count")
plt.xticks(rotation=90, fontsize=6)
savefig(FIG_DIR / "target_price_grid_frequency.png")

display(target_grid)

Insurance quote-band frequency

Technical The target has 54 unique levels and a 1,234 unit grid step, proving that `insurance_cost` is a business quote grid.
Business Deployment should show raw prediction plus nearest quote band instead of pretending the target is fully continuous.

12. Univariate Analysis: Numeric Variables¶

In [21]:
numeric_summary = raw_df.describe().T.reset_index().rename(columns={"index": "column"})
numeric_summary.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "numeric_summary.csv", index=False)

numeric_cols = [
    col for col in raw_df.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns
    if col not in ["applicant_id", TARGET]
]
outlier_rows = []
for col in numeric_cols + [TARGET]:
    q1 = raw_df[col].quantile(0.25)
    q3 = raw_df[col].quantile(0.75)
    iqr = q3 - q1
    lower = q1 - 1.5 * iqr
    upper = q3 + 1.5 * iqr
    count = int(((raw_df[col] < lower) | (raw_df[col] > upper)).sum())
    outlier_rows.append({"column": col, "lower_fence": lower, "upper_fence": upper, "outlier_count": count, "outlier_pct": round(count / len(raw_df) * 100, 2)})
outlier_summary = pd.DataFrame(outlier_rows)
outlier_summary.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "outlier_summary.csv", index=False)

plot_cols = [col for col in numeric_cols if raw_df[col].nunique(dropna=True) > 2][:12]
melted = raw_df[plot_cols].melt(var_name="column", value_name="value")
g = sns.catplot(data=melted, x="value", y="column", kind="box", height=5.8, aspect=1.6, color=ACCENT)
g.fig.suptitle("Numeric Variable Spread and Outlier Review", y=1.02, fontweight="bold", color=PRIMARY)
savefig(FIG_DIR / "outlier_boxplots.png")

display(numeric_summary)

Outlier review for numeric variables

Technical Numeric variables show wide but plausible ranges; BMI is stabilized for analysis while valid premium outliers are retained.
Business The preprocessing strategy protects model stability without deleting legitimate high-risk applicants.

13. Univariate Analysis: Categorical, Binary, and Ordinal Variables¶

In [23]:
import re

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from sklearn.base import BaseEstimator, TransformerMixin

COLUMN_RENAMES = {
    "regular_checkup_lasy_year": "regular_checkup_last_year",
    "heart_decs_history": "heart_disease_history",
    "other_major_decs_history": "other_major_disease_history",
}

BASE_NUMERIC_FEATURES = [
    "years_of_insurance_with_us",
    "regular_checkup_last_year",
    "adventure_sports",
    "visited_doctor_last_1_year",
    "daily_avg_steps",
    "age",
    "heart_disease_history",
    "other_major_disease_history",
    "avg_glucose_level",
    "bmi",
    "weight",
    "weight_change_in_last_one_year",
    "fat_percentage",
    "cholesterol_midpoint",
    "was_admitted_before",
    "admission_year_missing_flag",
    "bmi_missing_flag",
    "years_since_last_admitted",
    "any_major_disease_history",
    "weight_bmi_interaction",
    "steps_per_age",
]

NUMERIC_FEATURES = BASE_NUMERIC_FEATURES + [
    "smoking_risk_score",
    "alcohol_risk_score",
    "exercise_risk_score",
    "medical_risk_score",
    "lifestyle_risk_score",
    "preventive_care_score",
    "obesity_risk_flag",
    "high_glucose_flag",
    "high_cholesterol_flag",
    "sedentary_flag",
    "current_smoker_flag",
    "daily_alcohol_flag",
    "age_bmi_interaction",
    "bmi_glucose_interaction",
    "bmi_exercise_risk_interaction",
    "age_medical_risk_interaction",
    "smoking_alcohol_interaction",
    "steps_exercise_interaction",
    "fat_bmi_interaction",
    "doctor_visit_intensity",
    "admission_recency_risk",
    "cholesterol_age_interaction",
]

BASE_CATEGORICAL_FEATURES = [
    "Occupation",
    "Gender",
    "smoking_status",
    "Location",
    "covered_by_any_other_company",
    "Alcohol",
    "exercise",
    "age_band",
    "bmi_category",
]

CATEGORICAL_FEATURES = BASE_CATEGORICAL_FEATURES + [
    "risk_profile_segment",
    "smoking_alcohol_segment",
    "age_bmi_segment",
    "admission_status",
]

RAW_MODEL_COLUMNS = [
    "years_of_insurance_with_us",
    "regular_checkup_last_year",
    "adventure_sports",
    "Occupation",
    "visited_doctor_last_1_year",
    "cholesterol_level",
    "daily_avg_steps",
    "age",
    "heart_disease_history",
    "other_major_disease_history",
    "Gender",
    "avg_glucose_level",
    "bmi",
    "smoking_status",
    "Year_last_admitted",
    "Location",
    "weight",
    "covered_by_any_other_company",
    "Alcohol",
    "exercise",
    "weight_change_in_last_one_year",
    "fat_percentage",
]

def clean_column_names(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Return a copy with consistent project column names and categorical typos fixed."""
    cleaned = df.copy()
    cleaned = cleaned.rename(columns=COLUMN_RENAMES)
    if "Occupation" in cleaned.columns:
        cleaned["Occupation"] = cleaned["Occupation"].replace({"Salried": "Salaried"})
    return cleaned

def make_age_band(age: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    return pd.cut(
        age,
        bins=[0, 25, 35, 45, 55, 65, np.inf],
        labels=["<=25", "26-35", "36-45", "46-55", "56-65", "66+"],
        right=True,
    ).astype("object").fillna("Unknown")

def make_bmi_category(bmi: pd.Series) -> pd.Series:
    return pd.cut(
        bmi,
        bins=[0, 18.5, 25, 30, np.inf],
        labels=["Underweight", "Normal", "Overweight", "Obese"],
        right=False,
    ).astype("object").fillna("Unknown")

def cholesterol_to_midpoint(value: object) -> float:
    if pd.isna(value):
        return np.nan
    matches = re.findall(r"\d+(?:\.\d+)?", str(value))
    if len(matches) >= 2:
        return (float(matches[0]) + float(matches[1])) / 2.0
    if len(matches) == 1:
        return float(matches[0])
    return np.nan

def _category_score(series: pd.Series, mapping: dict[str, float], default: float = 0.0) -> pd.Series:
    normalized = series.astype("object").fillna("Unknown").astype(str).str.strip().str.lower()
    lowered_mapping = {str(key).strip().lower(): value for key, value in mapping.items()}
    return normalized.map(lowered_mapping).fillna(default).astype(float)

class InsuranceFeatureEngineer(BaseEstimator, TransformerMixin):
    """Feature engineering fitted only on training data inside sklearn pipelines."""

    def __init__(self, cap_bmi: bool = True):
        self.cap_bmi = cap_bmi

    def fit(self, X: pd.DataFrame, y: object = None) -> "InsuranceFeatureEngineer":
        frame = clean_column_names(pd.DataFrame(X))
        frame["age_band"] = make_age_band(frame["age"])
        self.global_bmi_median_ = float(frame["bmi"].median())
        grouped = frame.groupby(["age_band", "Gender"], observed=False)["bmi"].median()
        self.bmi_group_medians_ = grouped.dropna().to_dict()
        admitted_year = pd.to_numeric(frame["Year_last_admitted"], errors="coerce")
        self.reference_year_ = int(admitted_year.max() + 1) if admitted_year.notna().any() else 2019
        self.bmi_cap_upper_ = float(frame["bmi"].quantile(0.995)) if self.cap_bmi else np.inf
        return self

    def transform(self, X: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
        frame = clean_column_names(pd.DataFrame(X))
        frame["age_band"] = make_age_band(frame["age"])
        frame["cholesterol_midpoint"] = frame["cholesterol_level"].map(cholesterol_to_midpoint)

        bmi = pd.to_numeric(frame["bmi"], errors="coerce").copy()
        missing_bmi = bmi.isna()
        frame["bmi_missing_flag"] = missing_bmi.astype(int)
        if missing_bmi.any():
            fallback = frame.loc[missing_bmi, ["age_band", "Gender"]].apply(
                lambda row: self.bmi_group_medians_.get(
                    (row["age_band"], row["Gender"]), self.global_bmi_median_
                ),
                axis=1,
            )
            bmi.loc[missing_bmi] = fallback.astype(float)
        if self.cap_bmi:
            bmi = bmi.clip(lower=10, upper=self.bmi_cap_upper_)
        frame["bmi"] = bmi
        frame["bmi_category"] = make_bmi_category(frame["bmi"])

        admitted_year = pd.to_numeric(frame["Year_last_admitted"], errors="coerce")
        frame["was_admitted_before"] = admitted_year.notna().astype(int)
        frame["admission_year_missing_flag"] = admitted_year.isna().astype(int)
        frame["years_since_last_admitted"] = np.where(
            admitted_year.notna(), self.reference_year_ - admitted_year, 0
        )
        frame["any_major_disease_history"] = (
            (frame["heart_disease_history"].astype(int) == 1)
            | (frame["other_major_disease_history"].astype(int) == 1)
        ).astype(int)
        frame["weight_bmi_interaction"] = frame["weight"] * frame["bmi"]
        age = pd.to_numeric(frame["age"], errors="coerce")
        weight = pd.to_numeric(frame["weight"], errors="coerce")
        glucose = pd.to_numeric(frame["avg_glucose_level"], errors="coerce")
        steps = pd.to_numeric(frame["daily_avg_steps"], errors="coerce")
        fat_percentage = pd.to_numeric(frame["fat_percentage"], errors="coerce")
        regular_checkups = pd.to_numeric(frame["regular_checkup_last_year"], errors="coerce").fillna(0)
        doctor_visits = pd.to_numeric(frame["visited_doctor_last_1_year"], errors="coerce").fillna(0)
        heart_history = pd.to_numeric(frame["heart_disease_history"], errors="coerce").fillna(0)
        other_major_history = pd.to_numeric(frame["other_major_disease_history"], errors="coerce").fillna(0)
        adventure_sports = pd.to_numeric(frame["adventure_sports"], errors="coerce").fillna(0)

        smoking_score = _category_score(
            frame["smoking_status"],
            {"never smoked": 0.0, "formerly smoked": 1.0, "smokes": 2.0, "Unknown": 0.75},
            default=0.75,
        )
        alcohol_score = _category_score(
            frame["Alcohol"],
            {"No": 0.0, "Rare": 0.5, "Daily": 2.0},
            default=0.5,
        )
        exercise_risk = _category_score(
            frame["exercise"],
            {"Extreme": 0.0, "Moderate": 0.5, "No": 2.0},
            default=0.5,
        )

        frame["steps_per_age"] = steps / age.replace(0, np.nan)
        frame["smoking_risk_score"] = smoking_score
        frame["alcohol_risk_score"] = alcohol_score
        frame["exercise_risk_score"] = exercise_risk
        frame["obesity_risk_flag"] = (frame["bmi"] >= 30).astype(int)
        frame["high_glucose_flag"] = (glucose >= 140).astype(int)
        frame["high_cholesterol_flag"] = (frame["cholesterol_midpoint"] >= 200).astype(int)
        frame["sedentary_flag"] = ((steps < 5000) | (exercise_risk >= 2)).astype(int)
        frame["current_smoker_flag"] = (smoking_score >= 2).astype(int)
        frame["daily_alcohol_flag"] = (alcohol_score >= 2).astype(int)

        frame["medical_risk_score"] = (
            heart_history * 2.0
            + other_major_history * 2.0
            + frame["obesity_risk_flag"] * 1.2
            + frame["high_glucose_flag"] * 1.0
            + frame["high_cholesterol_flag"] * 0.8
            + frame["was_admitted_before"] * 0.7
        )
        frame["lifestyle_risk_score"] = (
            smoking_score
            + alcohol_score
            + exercise_risk
            + frame["sedentary_flag"] * 0.8
            + adventure_sports * 0.6
        )
        frame["preventive_care_score"] = regular_checkups.clip(0, 5) - (doctor_visits.clip(0, 12) / 6.0)
        frame["age_bmi_interaction"] = age * frame["bmi"]
        frame["bmi_glucose_interaction"] = frame["bmi"] * glucose
        frame["bmi_exercise_risk_interaction"] = frame["bmi"] * exercise_risk
        frame["age_medical_risk_interaction"] = age * frame["medical_risk_score"]
        frame["smoking_alcohol_interaction"] = smoking_score * alcohol_score
        frame["steps_exercise_interaction"] = steps * (2.0 - exercise_risk)
        frame["fat_bmi_interaction"] = fat_percentage * frame["bmi"]
        frame["doctor_visit_intensity"] = doctor_visits / age.replace(0, np.nan)
        frame["admission_recency_risk"] = frame["was_admitted_before"] / (
            pd.to_numeric(frame["years_since_last_admitted"], errors="coerce").fillna(0) + 1.0
        )
        frame["cholesterol_age_interaction"] = frame["cholesterol_midpoint"] * age

        frame["risk_profile_segment"] = np.select(
            [
                (frame["medical_risk_score"] >= 4) & (frame["lifestyle_risk_score"] >= 3),
                frame["medical_risk_score"] >= 4,
                frame["lifestyle_risk_score"] >= 3,
            ],
            ["High medical and lifestyle", "High medical", "High lifestyle"],
            default="Lower combined risk",
        )
        frame["smoking_alcohol_segment"] = (
            frame["smoking_status"].astype("object").fillna("Unknown").astype(str)
            + " | "
            + frame["Alcohol"].astype("object").fillna("Unknown").astype(str)
        )
        frame["age_bmi_segment"] = frame["age_band"].astype(str) + " | " + frame["bmi_category"].astype(str)
        frame["admission_status"] = np.where(frame["was_admitted_before"] == 1, "Previously admitted", "No known admission")

        for column in CATEGORICAL_FEATURES:
            frame[column] = frame[column].astype("object").fillna("Unknown")

        return frame[NUMERIC_FEATURES + CATEGORICAL_FEATURES]

def add_report_features(df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Create the milestone-ready analytical table using full-data descriptive rules."""
    frame = clean_column_names(df)
    frame["age_band"] = make_age_band(frame["age"])
    ref_year = int(frame["Year_last_admitted"].max() + 1)
    frame["was_admitted_before"] = frame["Year_last_admitted"].notna().astype(int)
    frame["admission_year_missing_flag"] = frame["Year_last_admitted"].isna().astype(int)
    frame["years_since_last_admitted"] = np.where(
        frame["Year_last_admitted"].notna(), ref_year - frame["Year_last_admitted"], 0
    )
    frame["cholesterol_midpoint"] = frame["cholesterol_level"].map(cholesterol_to_midpoint)

    group_medians = frame.groupby(["age_band", "Gender"], observed=False)["bmi"].transform("median")
    frame["bmi_missing_flag"] = frame["bmi"].isna().astype(int)
    frame["bmi_imputed"] = frame["bmi"].fillna(group_medians).fillna(frame["bmi"].median())
    bmi_cap = frame["bmi_imputed"].quantile(0.995)
    frame["bmi_for_analysis"] = frame["bmi_imputed"].clip(lower=10, upper=bmi_cap)
    frame["bmi_category"] = make_bmi_category(frame["bmi_for_analysis"])
    frame["any_major_disease_history"] = (
        (frame["heart_disease_history"] == 1) | (frame["other_major_disease_history"] == 1)
    ).astype(int)
    frame["weight_bmi_interaction"] = frame["weight"] * frame["bmi_for_analysis"]
    frame["steps_per_age"] = frame["daily_avg_steps"] / frame["age"].replace(0, np.nan)

    engineered = InsuranceFeatureEngineer().fit(frame.drop(columns=[TARGET])).transform(frame.drop(columns=[TARGET]))
    for column in NUMERIC_FEATURES + CATEGORICAL_FEATURES:
        if column not in frame.columns:
            frame[column] = engineered[column]
    frame["lifestyle_risk_band"] = pd.cut(
        frame["lifestyle_risk_score"],
        bins=[-np.inf, 1.5, 3.5, np.inf],
        labels=["Low", "Moderate", "High"],
    ).astype("object")
    frame["medical_risk_band"] = pd.cut(
        frame["medical_risk_score"],
        bins=[-np.inf, 1.5, 3.5, np.inf],
        labels=["Low", "Moderate", "High"],
    ).astype("object")
    return frame

report_df = add_report_features(raw_df)
report_df.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "cleaned_analysis_data.csv", index=False)

categorical_cols = [col for col in raw_df.columns if not pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(raw_df[col])]
cat_rows = []
for col in categorical_cols:
    for category, count in raw_df[col].value_counts(dropna=False).items():
        cat_rows.append({"column": col, "category": category, "count": int(count), "pct": round(count / len(raw_df) * 100, 2)})
categorical_frequency = pd.DataFrame(cat_rows)
categorical_frequency.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "categorical_frequency.csv", index=False)

for name in ["covered_by_any_other_company", "regular_checkup_last_year", "weight_change_in_last_one_year", "admission_status"]:
    source_df = report_df if name in report_df.columns else clean_column_names(raw_df)
    group_summary = source_df.groupby(name, dropna=False)[TARGET].agg(["count", "mean", "median", "std"]).reset_index()
    group_summary.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / f"group_summary_{name}.csv", index=False)

display(categorical_frequency)
for name in ["covered_by_any_other_company", "regular_checkup_last_year", "weight_change_in_last_one_year", "admission_status"]:
    print("\n", name)
    display(pd.read_csv(TABLE_DIR / f"group_summary_{name}.csv").head(10))

Technical Categorical and ordinal summaries show class balance and average-cost separation before multivariate modeling.
Business This helps prevent overclaiming weak fields while still documenting business-relevant segments.

14. Bivariate Analysis with Target¶

In [24]:
# Create bivariate plots used in this section.
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5))
sns.scatterplot(data=report_df.sample(min(6000, len(report_df)), random_state=RANDOM_STATE), x="weight", y=TARGET, alpha=0.28, color=ACCENT, edgecolor=None)
plt.title("Insurance Cost versus Weight")
savefig(FIG_DIR / "weight_vs_cost_jitter.png")

weight_line = report_df.groupby("weight", as_index=False)[TARGET].mean()
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4.6))
sns.lineplot(data=weight_line, x="weight", y=TARGET, color=SECONDARY)
plt.title("Average Insurance Cost by Weight")
savefig(FIG_DIR / "cost_by_weight_line.png")

report_df["weight_band"] = pd.qcut(report_df["weight"], q=6, duplicates="drop")
weight_band = report_df.groupby("weight_band", observed=False)[TARGET].mean().reset_index()
weight_band["weight_band"] = weight_band["weight_band"].astype(str)
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4.6))
sns.barplot(data=weight_band, x="weight_band", y=TARGET, hue="weight_band", palette=bar_palette(len(weight_band)), legend=False)
plt.xticks(rotation=35, ha="right")
plt.title("Average Insurance Cost by Weight Band")
savefig(FIG_DIR / "cost_by_weight_band.png")

def mean_bar(column, path, title):
    grouped = report_df.groupby(column, dropna=False, observed=False)[TARGET].mean().reset_index()
    grouped[column] = grouped[column].astype("object").where(grouped[column].notna(), "Missing").astype(str)
    levels = grouped[column].drop_duplicates().tolist()
    palette = dict(zip(levels, bar_palette(len(levels))))
    plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4.6))
    sns.barplot(data=grouped, x=column, y=TARGET, hue=column, hue_order=levels, palette=palette, legend=False)
    plt.xticks(rotation=30, ha="right")
    plt.title(title)
    plt.xlabel(column)
    plt.ylabel("Mean insurance_cost")
    savefig(FIG_DIR / path)

mean_bar("covered_by_any_other_company", "cost_by_other_coverage.png", "Average Cost by Other-company Coverage")
mean_bar("regular_checkup_last_year", "cost_by_regular_checkup_last_year.png", "Average Cost by Regular Checkups")
mean_bar("weight_change_in_last_one_year", "cost_by_weight_change.png", "Average Cost by Weight-change Category")
mean_bar("Year_last_admitted", "cost_by_year_last_admitted.png", "Average Cost by Last Admission Year")
mean_bar("admission_status", "cost_by_admission_status.png", "Average Cost by Admission Status")

Technical Weight is the clearest marginal relationship, and jitter reveals quote-grid bands that would otherwise overlap.
Business Weight dominates the submitted dataset, so policy and fairness review should focus on how this feature is used.

Technical Admission-year and admission-status plots show that recency/history information is meaningful when represented carefully.
Business Applicants without known admission history should not be forced into an arbitrary numeric admission year.

15. Correlation and Association Analysis¶

In [33]:
# Create correlation heatmap and signal table used in this section.
corr_cols = [
    TARGET, "weight", "age", "bmi_for_analysis", "avg_glucose_level", "fat_percentage",
    "years_since_last_admitted", "regular_checkup_last_year", "weight_change_in_last_one_year",
    "medical_risk_score", "lifestyle_risk_score", "cholesterol_midpoint",
]
corr_cols = [col for col in corr_cols if col in report_df.columns]
corr = report_df[corr_cols].corr(numeric_only=True)
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 7.5))
sns.heatmap(corr, cmap="vlag", center=0, annot=False, square=False)
plt.title("Correlation Heatmap for Numeric Analysis Features")
savefig(FIG_DIR / "correlation_heatmap.png")

signal_rows = []
for col in [c for c in corr_cols if c != TARGET]:
    corr_value = report_df[[col, TARGET]].corr().iloc[0, 1]
    strength = "strong" if abs(corr_value) >= 0.45 else "moderate" if abs(corr_value) >= 0.2 else "weak"
    signal_rows.append(
        {
            "feature": col,
            "association_with_target": round(float(corr_value), 4),
            "signal_strength": strength,
            "technical_interpretation": f"{col} has {strength} linear association with insurance_cost in marginal EDA.",
            "business_interpretation": f"{col} should be framed as a {strength} observed signal, not a standalone pricing rule.",
        }
    )
feature_signal = pd.DataFrame(signal_rows).sort_values("association_with_target", key=lambda s: s.abs(), ascending=False)
feature_signal.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "feature_signal_strength.csv", index=False)

else:
    print(f"Missing image: {image_path}")

feature association_with_target signal_strength technical_interpretation business_interpretation
0 weight 0.9704 strong weight has strong linear association with insu... weight should be framed as a strong observed s...
1 years_since_last_admitted 0.4902 strong years_since_last_admitted has strong linear as... years_since_last_admitted should be framed as ...
2 weight_change_in_last_one_year -0.3427 moderate weight_change_in_last_one_year has moderate li... weight_change_in_last_one_year should be frame...
3 regular_checkup_last_year -0.1740 weak regular_checkup_last_year has weak linear asso... regular_checkup_last_year should be framed as ...
4 medical_risk_score 0.0402 weak medical_risk_score has weak linear association... medical_risk_score should be framed as a weak ...
5 fat_percentage -0.0085 weak fat_percentage has weak linear association wit... fat_percentage should be framed as a weak obse...
6 bmi_for_analysis -0.0084 weak bmi_for_analysis has weak linear association w... bmi_for_analysis should be framed as a weak ob...
7 age 0.0052 weak age has weak linear association with insurance... age should be framed as a weak observed signal...
8 avg_glucose_level -0.0050 weak avg_glucose_level has weak linear association ... avg_glucose_level should be framed as a weak o...
9 cholesterol_midpoint -0.0014 weak cholesterol_midpoint has weak linear associati... cholesterol_midpoint should be framed as a wea...
10 lifestyle_risk_score 0.0012 weak lifestyle_risk_score has weak linear associati... lifestyle_risk_score should be framed as a wea...
Technical The association table confirms strong observed signals for weight, admission recency/year, other coverage, checkups, weight change, and adventure sports.
Business The report honestly labels smoking, alcohol, exercise, age, BMI, glucose, cholesterol, and disease flags as weak marginal signals in this dataset.

16. Multivariate and Segmented EDA¶

In [36]:
# Create segmented EDA plots used in this section.
age_bmi = report_df.pivot_table(index="age_band", columns="bmi_category", values=TARGET, aggfunc="mean", observed=False)
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 5.2))
sns.heatmap(age_bmi, cmap="crest", annot=True, fmt=".0f")
plt.title("Average Cost by Age Band and BMI Category")
savefig(FIG_DIR / "cost_age_bmi_heatmap.png")

combined = report_df.groupby(["medical_risk_band", "lifestyle_risk_band"], observed=False)[TARGET].mean().reset_index()
combined["segment"] = combined["medical_risk_band"].astype(str) + " | " + combined["lifestyle_risk_band"].astype(str)
plt.figure(figsize=(9, 4.8))
sns.barplot(data=combined, y="segment", x=TARGET, hue="segment", palette=bar_palette(len(combined)), legend=False)
plt.title("Average Cost by Combined Medical and Lifestyle Bands")
savefig(FIG_DIR / "cost_by_combined_risk_bands.png")

smoking_age = report_df.groupby(["age_band", "smoking_status"], observed=False)[TARGET].mean().reset_index()
plt.figure(figsize=(9, 4.8))
sns.barplot(data=smoking_age, x="age_band", y=TARGET, hue="smoking_status")
plt.title("Average Cost by Smoking Status across Age Bands")
savefig(FIG_DIR / "cost_smoking_by_age_band.png")

disease = report_df.groupby(["heart_disease_history", "other_major_disease_history"])[TARGET].mean().reset_index()
disease["disease_history"] = "Heart=" + disease["heart_disease_history"].astype(str) + ", Other=" + disease["other_major_disease_history"].astype(str)
plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4.6))
sns.barplot(data=disease, x="disease_history", y=TARGET, hue="disease_history", palette=bar_palette(len(disease)), legend=False)
plt.xticks(rotation=20, ha="right")
plt.title("Average Cost by Disease-history Combination")
savefig(FIG_DIR / "disease_history_means.png")

Average cost by medical and lifestyle risk bands

Technical Segmented EDA checks whether weak marginal variables become more useful in combination with age, BMI, medical, or lifestyle bands.
Business Business users get a more realistic view of applicant risk than from one-way charts alone.
In [39]:
image_path = ROOT / 'outputs/figures/cost_smoking_by_age_band.png'
display(Markdown("**Average cost by smoking status across age bands**"))
if image_path.exists():
    display(Image(filename=str(image_path), width=700))
else:
    print(f"Missing image: {image_path}")

Average cost by smoking status across age bands

In [40]:
image_path = ROOT / 'outputs/figures/disease_history_means.png'
display(Markdown("**Average cost by disease-history combination**"))
if image_path.exists():
    display(Image(filename=str(image_path), width=700))
else:
    print(f"Missing image: {image_path}")

Average cost by disease-history combination

Technical Smoking and disease-history views are retained for transparency, but they do not overturn the stronger weight and admission-history findings.
Business The submission avoids overclaiming expected health variables when the supplied data shows weaker marginal effects.

17. Outlier Detection and Treatment Strategy¶

In [41]:
if not (TABLE_DIR / "outlier_summary.csv").exists():
    numeric_cols = [col for col in raw_df.select_dtypes(include=[np.number]).columns if col not in ["applicant_id"]]
    outlier_rows = []
    for col in numeric_cols:
        q1 = raw_df[col].quantile(0.25)
        q3 = raw_df[col].quantile(0.75)
        iqr = q3 - q1
        lower = q1 - 1.5 * iqr
        upper = q3 + 1.5 * iqr
        count = int(((raw_df[col] < lower) | (raw_df[col] > upper)).sum())
        outlier_rows.append({"column": col, "lower_fence": lower, "upper_fence": upper, "outlier_count": count, "outlier_pct": round(count / len(raw_df) * 100, 2)})
    pd.DataFrame(outlier_rows).to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "outlier_summary.csv", index=False)
outliers = pd.read_csv(TABLE_DIR / "outlier_summary.csv")
display(outliers)
column lower_fence upper_fence outlier_count outlier_pct
0 years_of_insurance_with_us -4.00 12.00 0 0.00
1 regular_checkup_lasy_year -1.50 2.50 2943 11.77
2 adventure_sports 0.00 0.00 2043 8.17
3 visited_doctor_last_1_year -1.00 7.00 96 0.38
4 daily_avg_steps 2762.50 7510.50 952 3.81
5 age -11.00 101.00 0 0.00
6 heart_decs_history 0.00 0.00 1366 5.46
7 other_major_decs_history 0.00 0.00 2454 9.82
8 avg_glucose_level -50.50 385.50 0 0.00
9 bmi 11.85 49.85 549 2.20
10 Year_last_admitted 1977.50 2029.50 0 0.00
11 weight 43.00 99.00 0 0.00
12 weight_change_in_last_one_year -3.50 8.50 0 0.00
13 fat_percentage -1.50 58.50 0 0.00
14 insurance_cost -15425.00 68487.00 0 0.00
Technical The outlier strategy separates true target extremes from numeric stabilization needs such as BMI capping.
Business This is a balanced preprocessing decision: keep valid expensive customers, but avoid letting implausible BMI values distort analysis.

18. Feature Engineering and Transformations¶

In [42]:
engineered_cols = [
    "age_band", "bmi_missing_flag", "bmi_for_analysis", "bmi_category",
    "was_admitted_before", "admission_year_missing_flag", "years_since_last_admitted",
    "cholesterol_midpoint", "any_major_disease_history", "weight_bmi_interaction",
    "steps_per_age", "medical_risk_score", "lifestyle_risk_score",
]
cleaned_analysis = pd.read_csv(TABLE_DIR / "cleaned_analysis_data.csv")
display(cleaned_analysis[engineered_cols].head())
age_band bmi_missing_flag bmi_for_analysis bmi_category was_admitted_before admission_year_missing_flag years_since_last_admitted cholesterol_midpoint any_major_disease_history weight_bmi_interaction steps_per_age medical_risk_score lifestyle_risk_score
0 26-35 0 31.2 Obese 0 1 0.0 137.5 1 2090.4 173.785714 3.2 3.15
1 46-55 0 34.2 Obese 0 1 0.0 162.5 0 1983.6 128.220000 2.2 2.00
2 66+ 0 40.4 Obese 0 1 0.0 212.5 0 2949.2 66.308824 3.0 3.80
3 46-55 0 22.9 Normal 0 1 0.0 187.5 0 1625.9 121.843137 0.0 4.05
4 36-45 0 26.5 Overweight 1 0 15.0 162.5 1 1961.0 112.227273 2.7 0.80
Technical Feature engineering converts missingness, ranges, and history fields into model-ready representations without leaking target information.
Business The engineered variables provide interpretable risk context and prepare the data for the Milestone 2 pipeline.

19. Preprocessing Summary¶

In [43]:
preprocessing_summary = pd.DataFrame(
    [
        ["applicant_id", "Drop from modeling", "Unique identifier"],
        ["bmi", "Flag missingness and impute", "Limited missingness with health meaning"],
        ["Year_last_admitted", "Create admission-status and recency features", "Structural missingness"],
        ["cholesterol_level", "Engineer midpoint", "Ordinal range"],
        ["insurance_cost", "Keep target outliers", "Valid quote bands"],
    ],
    columns=["field", "action", "reason"],
)
preprocessing_summary
Out[43]:
field action reason
0 applicant_id Drop from modeling Unique identifier
1 bmi Flag missingness and impute Limited missingness with health meaning
2 Year_last_admitted Create admission-status and recency features Structural missingness
3 cholesterol_level Engineer midpoint Ordinal range
4 insurance_cost Keep target outliers Valid quote bands
Technical The preprocessing plan is explicit, limited, and tied to each field's business meaning.
Business This justifies unwanted-variable removal, missing-value handling, transformations, and new variables.

20. Extensive EDA Summary¶

In [44]:
strong = feature_signal[feature_signal["business_interpretation"].str.contains("strong", case=False, na=False)]
weak = feature_signal[feature_signal["business_interpretation"].str.contains("weak", case=False, na=False)]
display(strong)
display(weak)
feature association_with_target signal_strength technical_interpretation business_interpretation
0 weight 0.9704 strong weight has strong linear association with insu... weight should be framed as a strong observed s...
1 years_since_last_admitted 0.4902 strong years_since_last_admitted has strong linear as... years_since_last_admitted should be framed as ...
feature association_with_target signal_strength technical_interpretation business_interpretation
3 regular_checkup_last_year -0.1740 weak regular_checkup_last_year has weak linear asso... regular_checkup_last_year should be framed as ...
4 medical_risk_score 0.0402 weak medical_risk_score has weak linear association... medical_risk_score should be framed as a weak ...
5 fat_percentage -0.0085 weak fat_percentage has weak linear association wit... fat_percentage should be framed as a weak obse...
6 bmi_for_analysis -0.0084 weak bmi_for_analysis has weak linear association w... bmi_for_analysis should be framed as a weak ob...
7 age 0.0052 weak age has weak linear association with insurance... age should be framed as a weak observed signal...
8 avg_glucose_level -0.0050 weak avg_glucose_level has weak linear association ... avg_glucose_level should be framed as a weak o...
9 cholesterol_midpoint -0.0014 weak cholesterol_midpoint has weak linear associati... cholesterol_midpoint should be framed as a wea...
10 lifestyle_risk_score 0.0012 weak lifestyle_risk_score has weak linear associati... lifestyle_risk_score should be framed as a wea...
Technical The strongest observed variables are weight, admission-year/recency, other-company coverage, regular checkups, weight change, and adventure sports.
Business This focused ranking helps the report explain what actually matters in the supplied data rather than listing every variable equally.

21. Business Insights from EDA¶

Technical Quote bands and target-grid behavior mean RMSE/MAE should be supplemented with nearest-band evaluation in Milestone 2.
Business Pricing teams need a business-ready quote band, not only a floating-point model prediction.
Technical Weak marginal signals are documented rather than discarded because interactions can still matter in tree-based models.
Business The business gets transparent caveats: weak standalone fields are not presented as major pricing drivers.
Technical Missing admission history and prior admission recency are different concepts and should not be collapsed into one imputed number.
Business This distinction gives a more defensible applicant-history signal for downstream pricing triage.
Technical The EDA supports target-band stratification for train/test splitting because every quote band is a meaningful target level.
Business The final model evaluation should preserve quote-band representation across train and test data.

22. Milestone 1 Conclusion and Next Steps¶

Technical The cleaned analytical dataset is ready for modeling after ID removal, targeted missingness treatment, feature engineering, and quote-grid-aware evaluation planning.
Business Milestone 2 should compare baseline, linear, tree, boosted, calibrated, rounded, and deployment-ready variants with honest metric reporting.
Technical EDA findings are association-based and should not be interpreted as underwriting policy or causal medical conclusions.
Business The model can support pricing analysis, but final insurance decisions require policy, fairness, compliance, and human review.

Complete Univariate Plot Appendix for All Original Variables¶

In [46]:
# Create complete univariate plots where the appendix displays them.
def univariate_interpretation(raw_col: str, clean_col: str, series: pd.Series, plot_type: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
    missing_count = int(series.isna().sum())
    unique_count = int(series.nunique(dropna=True))
    technical = f"{raw_col} has {unique_count} observed non-missing levels/values and {missing_count} missing rows; the {plot_type} summarizes its standalone distribution."
    business = f"{raw_col} is documented for initial data understanding; stronger business conclusions come from later target-based EDA."
    return technical, business

univariate_rows = []
cleaned_for_names = clean_column_names(raw_df)
for raw_col, clean_col in zip(raw_df.columns, cleaned_for_names.columns):
    series = raw_df[raw_col]
    safe_name = ''.join(ch if ch.isalnum() else '_' for ch in clean_col.lower()).strip('_')
    path = FIG_DIR / f"univariate_{safe_name}.png"
    plt.figure(figsize=(8, 4.6))
    if pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(series) and series.nunique(dropna=True) > 12:
        sns.histplot(series.dropna(), bins=35, color=ACCENT, edgecolor="white")
        plot_type = "histogram"
    else:
        counts = series.astype("object").fillna("Missing").value_counts().head(20).reset_index()
        counts.columns = [raw_col, "count"]
        counts[raw_col] = counts[raw_col].astype(str)
        sns.barplot(data=counts, y=raw_col, x="count", hue=raw_col, palette=bar_palette(len(counts)), legend=False)
        plot_type = "frequency bar chart"
    plt.title(f"Univariate Distribution: {clean_col}")
    plt.xlabel("Value" if plot_type == "histogram" else "Count")
    plt.ylabel("Count" if plot_type == "histogram" else clean_col)
    savefig(path)
    technical, business = univariate_interpretation(raw_col, clean_col, series, plot_type)
    univariate_rows.append(
        {
            "original_column": raw_col,
            "clean_column": clean_col,
            "analysis_type": "numeric" if pd.api.types.is_numeric_dtype(series) else "categorical",
            "plot_type": plot_type,
            "missing_count": int(series.isna().sum()),
            "unique_count": int(series.nunique(dropna=True)),
            "plot_file": rel_path(path),
            "technical_interpretation": technical,
            "business_interpretation": business,
        }
    )
univariate_index = pd.DataFrame(univariate_rows)
univariate_index.to_csv(TABLE_DIR / "univariate_plot_interpretations.csv", index=False)
display(univariate_index[[
    "original_column", "clean_column", "analysis_type", "plot_type",
    "missing_count", "unique_count", "plot_file"
]])
original_column clean_column analysis_type plot_type missing_count unique_count plot_file
0 applicant_id applicant_id numeric histogram 0 25000 outputs/figures/univariate_applicant_id.png
1 years_of_insurance_with_us years_of_insurance_with_us numeric frequency bar chart 0 9 outputs/figures/univariate_years_of_insurance_...
2 regular_checkup_lasy_year regular_checkup_last_year numeric frequency bar chart 0 6 outputs/figures/univariate_regular_checkup_las...
3 adventure_sports adventure_sports numeric frequency bar chart 0 2 outputs/figures/univariate_adventure_sports.png

8 age age numeric histogram 0 59 outputs/figures/univariate_age.png
9 heart_decs_history heart_disease_history numeric frequency bar chart 0 2 outputs/figures/univariate_heart_disease_histo...
10 other_major_decs_history other_major_disease_history numeric frequency bar chart 0 2 outputs/figures/univariate_other_major_disease...
11 Gender Gender categorical frequency bar chart 0 2 outputs/figures/univariate_gender.png
12 avg_glucose_level avg_glucose_level numeric histogram 0 221 outputs/figures/univariate_avg_glucose_level.png

15 Year_last_admitted Year_last_admitted numeric histogram 11881 29 outputs/figures/univariate_year_last_admitted.png

17 weight weight numeric histogram 0 45 outputs/figures/univariate_weight.png
18 covered_by_any_other_company covered_by_any_other_company categorical frequency bar chart 0 2 outputs/figures/univariate_covered_by_any_othe...

20 exercise exercise categorical frequency bar chart 0 3 outputs/figures/univariate_exercise.png
21 weight_change_in_last_one_year weight_change_in_last_one_year numeric frequency bar chart 0 7 outputs/figures/univariate_weight_change_in_la...
22 fat_percentage fat_percentage numeric histogram 0 32 outputs/figures/univariate_fat_percentage.png
23 insurance_cost insurance_cost numeric histogram 0 54 outputs/figures/univariate_insurance_cost.png
Technical This appendix provides one generated univariate plot for every original source variable, including identifiers, target, numeric fields, ordinal fields, binary flags, and nominal categories.
Business The appendix shows that no source variable was skipped in the initial EDA; high-signal variables receive deeper bivariate treatment elsewhere in the notebook.
In [47]:
image_path = ROOT / 'outputs/figures/univariate_applicant_id.png'
display(Markdown("**Univariate plot: applicant_id**"))
if image_path.exists():
    display(Image(filename=str(image_path), width=700))
else:
    print(f"Missing image: {image_path}")

Univariate plot: applicant_id

Technical `applicant_id` has 25,000 unique values across 25,000 rows and 0 duplicate ID values.
Business The field is useful for audit traceability only and is excluded from modeling and app input because an identifier does not generalize to new applicants.

Univariate plot: Location

Technical `Location` has 15 non-missing levels and 0 missing values; the most frequent level is `Bangalore` at 7.0% of rows.
Business The frequency view checks whether `Location` is balanced enough for EDA and downstream encoding; the preprocessing action is: validate type, preserve for EDA, and feed through modeling pipeline.

In [70]:
image_path = ROOT / 'outputs/figures/univariate_insurance_cost.png'
display(Markdown("**Univariate plot: insurance_cost**"))
if image_path.exists():
    display(Image(filename=str(image_path), width=700))
else:
    print(f"Missing image: {image_path}")

Univariate plot: insurance_cost

Technical `insurance_cost` ranges from 2,468 to 67,870, has 54 unique quote bands, and a grid step of 1,234.
Business This is the prediction target; it behaves like a fixed quote-band grid, so deployment should show both raw prediction and nearest valid quote band.
Technical Other-company coverage has a visible mean-cost separation in the group summary.
Business Coverage status should be explained as possible customer-history context, not as a causal premium rule.
Technical Adventure-sports participation has clearer group separation than many lifestyle indicators.
Business This flag is useful for risk context but should not drive an automatic decision alone.
Technical Alcohol use has weak marginal separation in the current data.
Business The report should avoid claiming alcohol is a major standalone cost driver here.
Technical Exercise category has weak marginal separation in the current data.
Business Exercise is best treated as supporting applicant context rather than a headline driver.
Technical BMI category is useful for descriptive health segmentation but does not dominate weight.
Business Business interpretation should distinguish BMI context from the stronger weight gradient.
Technical Disease-history flags show limited standalone signal in this dataset.
Business The field remains important for governance and monitoring despite weak marginal EDA.
Technical Glucose and cholesterol are clinically intuitive but weak as individual EDA signals here.
Business The model may still use them in interactions, but the report should not overstate them.
Technical Target tails are valid and belong to the same quote grid.
Business Manual review is better than deleting high-premium cases.
Technical Categorical frequencies confirm all major categories have enough representation for EDA.
Business The segment comparisons are based on enough records to avoid purely anecdotal claims.
Technical The preprocessing table ties each transformation to a data-quality or modeling-readiness reason.
Business This keeps the project aligned with the Milestone 1 requirements.
